refactor(capabilities): adjust risk levels, rename capabilities, add Daytona docs (#675)

## What

- Change `virtual_bash` risk level from High to Low (in-memory sandboxed
execution)
- Change `web_fetch` risk level from High to Medium (network access but
SSRF-protected)
- Rename capability display names: Session Schedules → Schedules,
Session Storage → Storage, Agent Instructions → AGENTS.md
- Remove Risk column/row from all capability documentation pages
- Add `docs/capabilities/daytona.md` capability reference page

## Why

`virtual_bash` runs in-memory with no host/network access — High risk
was overly restrictive. `web_fetch` has SSRF protection so Medium is
more appropriate. Display name renames make the UI cleaner. Risk columns
in docs added noise without value. Daytona was missing from capability
docs.

## How

- Removed `risk_level()` override from `VirtualBashCapability` (defaults
to Low)
- Changed `web_fetch` risk level enum to `Medium`
- Updated `name()` return values in 3 capability structs
- Removed `| **Risk** |` rows from 15 doc pages via sed
- Updated specs/threat-model to reflect new high-risk list (docker,
daytona, codesandbox)
- Created Daytona capability reference doc page

## Risk
- Low
- Admin enforcement still applies to docker/daytona/codesandbox (High).
Non-admin users can now assign virtual_bash and web_fetch without admin
role.

## Checklist
- [x] Tests added or updated
- [x] Backward compatibility considered

fix: force firebase SW to activate immediately via skipWaiting

Without skipWaiting(), the updated firebase-messaging-sw.js waits for
all open tabs to close before taking over. The old SW (which read from
payload.notification — now undefined since we switched to data-only
messages) kept running, causing the fallback 'You have a new
notification' body to show instead of the actual content.

Adding install/activate listeners with skipWaiting()+clients.claim()
forces the new SW to take control on the next push, so the type-specific
formatting (title/body from payload.data) works immediately.

* fix: исправить миграции БД для EXE + серверный баг supervision

Корневая причина EXE: run_migrations() вызывался ДО initialize_database(),
ALTER TABLE падали на несуществующих таблицах, _migrations_completed=True
блокировал повторный запуск.

Серверная причина надзора: status_changed_date хранится как str,
но supervision_router вызывал .isoformat() → AttributeError → HTTP 500.

Изменения:
- db_manager.py: initialize_database() теперь вызывается ДО миграций
- migrations.py: CREATE TABLE включают ВСЕ текущие колонки
  (employees +6, contracts +52, crm_cards +8, stage_executors +1, salaries +6)

- supervision_router.py: str() вместо .isoformat() для status_changed_date

commit.message:
Revive --replace flag, make --force unconditional override

Split the old --force into two flags for safer sandbox replacement:
- --replace: replaces existing sandbox but errors if unapplied work exists
- --force: implies --replace, skips the safety check entirely

Enhanced unapplied work detection to catch both uncommitted changes AND
commits beyond the baseline SHA (previously only checked dirty working
tree via git status --porcelain). This affects destroy confirmation,
replace safety checks, and the HasChanges field shown in yoloai ls.

commit.message:
fix: add Railway buildCommand to build frontend on every deploy

Previously Railway had no buildCommand so it never rebuilt the frontend
from source — it only served whatever static files were committed to git.
This caused stale UI even after JSX changes were pushed.

Now Railway runs `npm install && npm run build` in frontend-src on every
deploy, outputting fresh assets to backend/static/. No more manual
static file commits needed.

fix: enterprise pipeline hardening — LLM timeout, rate limits, email security

C1 - Rate limits: raise defaults from 10/100 to 500/5000 per user/institution per hour
     (configurable via SUBMISSION_RATE_LIMIT_* env vars); supports thousands of
     concurrent students without hitting ceiling
C2 - OpenAI timeouts: add timeout=30s to chat.completions.create() and timeout=60s
     to audio.transcriptions.create() — prevents 160s task hang on LLM API stall
C3 - LLM_FAILED status: add STATUS_LLM_FAILED to AssessmentSubmission; transition
     evaluate_writing/evaluate_speaking to LLM_FAILED on final retry exhaustion —
     students see an error state instead of an infinite loading spinner
C4 - Webhook HMAC: add _verify_webhook_signature() to email_event_webhook with
     SendGrid HMAC-SHA256 verification; rejects forged bounce events with 403;
     EMAIL_WEBHOOK_SECRET env var controls (empty = dev-mode skip)
C5 - XSS fix: add |escape filter to {{ student_name }} in submission_ready.html
C7 - Email soft-limit: add explicit SoftTimeLimitExceeded handler to send_email_report
     (matches pattern already in generate_report) — records DLQ on SMTP hang
H3 - Bulk resend guard: bulk_resend_email defaults dry_run=True; admins must pass
     dry_run=false explicitly to prevent accidental 500-student email blast
H4 - ValueError → DLQ: remove ValueError from autoretry_for in evaluate_writing/
     evaluate_speaking; LLM parse errors go straight to DLQ (no wasted retries)
